mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month acceleration in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals resilient consumer demand, which supports corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is continuous and does not require a discrete trigger.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS 3-month momentum and forward 20d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or positive RSAFS momentum periods do not outperform negative momentum periods at p>0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% for at least two consecutive months, the cumulative tightening raises the discount rate on future equity cash flows and increases financing costs for leveraged firms, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days).
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns following FEDFUNDS>5.0% triggers are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC is not significantly negative (p>0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts (HOUST) and an elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly signal that monetary tightening is transmitting into real housing demand destruction, a leading indicator of broader economic slowdown that predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. Neither series alone captures this transmission; the interaction of falling housing activity under tight money is required.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST fell from 1850.0 to 1802.0 (revised) between vintages in mid-2006 while FEDFUNDS held at 5.24%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing downturn
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are non-negative in over 55% of joint-trigger instances, or the interaction term shows no incremental Rank IC improvement (<0.02) versus HOUST or FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where unemployment (UNRATE) is near cyclical lows but nonfarm payroll (PAYEMS) growth decelerates month-over-month, the labor market is signaling an approaching turning point. This deceleration under low-unemployment conditions predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets anticipate slowing growth ahead of unemployment increases.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5.0% and PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth decelerates by more than 1pp
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=4.7% revised as of 2006-07-01, PAYEMS=136529.0 revised
failure_condition: Positive 20d forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances during low-unemployment deceleration episodes, or Rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p>0.10) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding real output capacity and corporate revenue momentum, which is priced into equities with a short lag, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days. This is a continuous always-on trend signal without a discrete activation threshold.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO MoM growth and forward 5d SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or sign hit-rate does not exceed 52%.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth accelerates above a threshold consistent with entrenched inflation concern, markets price in expectations of further monetary tightening, compressing equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 55% of instances when CPI YoY exceeds 4.0%, or Rank IC fails to reach significance (p>0.10) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deterioration where nonfarm payroll (PAYEMS) growth turns negative while unemployment (UNRATE) simultaneously rises signals the onset of a labor-market-driven recession, which predicts significantly negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. The interaction is required because either series alone (a single weak payroll print, or a small unemployment tick) is noisy, but joint deterioration confirms regime change.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 1-month change < 0 and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE ticked from 4.8 to 4.7 revised while PAYEMS rose modestly as of mid-2006, indicating no current trigger but establishing baseline
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of joint-trigger instances, or the joint-trigger Rank IC does not exceed single-series Rank IC by at least 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS elevated and stable near cycle highs), a sharp single-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) is interpreted by markets as an early recession signal, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week (5 trading days) as risk-off repricing occurs quickly. Outside tightening regimes, the same HOUST decline is not expected to carry the same signal.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage_value=1850.0 revised to 1802.0 near FEDFUNDS=5.24% as of mid-2006
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following HOUST declines >5% during tightening regimes are non-negative in more than 55% of instances, or the effect disappears (Rank IC<0.02) once regime conditioning is removed, indicating no regime dependence.
